specification.topic-statement: The viewport rendering system calculates which portion of the document is visible given the current scroll offset and renders a complete terminal frame using the layer cake.

scope.in-scope: Document height calculation, viewport window computation, scrollback logical-to-physical row mapping, input buffer screen position calculation, layer cake viewport configuration, framebuffer construction with ANSI cursor control, cursor position calculation, framebuffer storage, and render timing.
scope.boundary: Individual layer visibility checks, height queries, and render calls are performed by the layer cake. The viewport system sets the viewport row and height on the layer cake and delegates rendering to it.
Physical-to-logical row mapping is computed by the scrollback system. Layout synchronization is requested before any calculations.
The framebuffer is written atomically to the terminal file descriptor. In headless mode (file descriptor is -1), write errors are suppressed.

field.name: scrollback_lines_count
field.note: number of logical scrollback lines to render

field.name: input_buffer_start_row
field.note: terminal row where input buffer begins; equals terminal_rows if off-screen

field.name: 5. Input buffer
field.note: 0 when hidden; 1 minimum when visible and empty

field.name: 6. Completion list
field.note: present only when completion is active

behavior.document-height-calculation: The total document height in rows is the sum of each layer's row count according to the ordering in the data contract above. The input buffer contributes either zero rows (when hidden), one row (when visible and empty), or the actual physical line count of its text. Scrollback contributes its total physical lines (accounting for line wrapping at the current terminal width).
behavior.viewport-window-computation:
Input buffer and scrollback layouts are synchronized to the current terminal width before any computation.
If the document height is less than or equal to the terminal height, the entire document is visible: the first visible row is 0 and the last visible row is document_height minus 1.
If the document height exceeds the terminal height: - The maximum scroll offset is document_height minus terminal_height. - The viewport offset is clamped to the maximum if it exceeds it. - The last visible row is document_height minus 1 minus the (clamped) offset. - The first visible row is last_visible_row plus 1 minus terminal_height.
A viewport offset of zero shows the bottom of the document (most recent content). Increasing the offset scrolls toward the top.
behavior.scrollback-visibility-mapping:
The document row where the scrollback region begins is determined from the banner height. The separator is at banner_rows plus total scrollback physical rows plus spinner rows.
If the first visible row is at or after the separator row, no scrollback is visible: scrollback_start_line and scrollback_lines_count are both zero.
Otherwise, the physical row offset within the scrollback region at the first visible document row is computed and converted to a logical line index by the scrollback system. The number of logical lines that fit in the visible range is counted from that starting index.
behavior.input-buffer-screen-position:
The input buffer begins one row after the separator in the document.
- If the input buffer start is within the visible range, its screen row is its document row minus the first visible document row. - If the input buffer start is before the first visible row (scrolled partially out of view), its screen row is zero. - If the input buffer start is after the last visible row (scrolled completely out of view), its screen row is set to terminal_height (a sentinel value indicating off-screen).
behavior.separator-visibility: The separator is visible if its document row falls within the range [first_visible_row, last_visible_row] inclusive.
behavior.state-based-visibility-overrides:
Before rendering, the current agent's state is read atomically and layer visibility flags are set:
- If the agent is dead: both the spinner and input buffer are hidden. - If the agent is in WAITING_FOR_LLM or EXECUTING_TOOL state: the spinner is shown and the input buffer is hidden. - If the agent is idle: the spinner is hidden and the input buffer visibility follows whether its screen position is on-screen.
These visibility values are written back to the agent's state fields, causing them to take effect immediately in the layer cake render.
behavior.layer-cake-rendering:
The layer cake's viewport row is set to the first visible document row and viewport height is set to the terminal height. The layer cake then renders by iterating through all layers in order: - Invisible layers are skipped. - Each visible layer's height is computed. - Only the portion of the layer overlapping the viewport window is rendered; start_row and row_count clip the layer to the visible range. - Rendering stops once past the viewport.
All rendered output is accumulated in an output buffer initialized to 4 KB.
behavior.trailing-crlf-removal: If the document fills or overflows the terminal height, and the output buffer ends with a carriage-return followed by a newline, those two bytes are removed. This prevents the terminal from auto-scrolling when the last rendered row is at the bottom of the screen.
behavior.cursor-position-calculation:
If the input buffer is visible and contains text, the cursor's screen coordinates are computed from the cursor byte offset within the text, the terminal width, and the input buffer's screen row from the viewport calculation. The resulting screen row and column are stored on the REPL context.
If the input buffer is visible but empty, the cursor is placed at the first column of the input buffer's screen row.
If the input buffer is hidden, the cursor coordinates are left at zero.
behavior.framebuffer-construction:
A framebuffer is built by concatenating: 1. Hide cursor escape sequence 2. Home cursor escape sequence (move to row 1, column 1) 3. The rendered layer cake output 4. Erase-to-end-of-screen sequence 5. Either show-cursor or hide-cursor sequence depending on whether the input buffer is visible 6. If input buffer is visible: cursor positioning escape sequence for the computed row and column
The complete framebuffer is written to the terminal file descriptor in a single write call. Write errors are returned unless the file descriptor is -1 (headless mode).
behavior.framebuffer-storage: The previous framebuffer is freed and replaced with the newly rendered one. The framebuffer length and cursor position are updated on the REPL context. These values are made available to the control socket for the read_framebuffer command.
behavior.render-timing: If a render-start timestamp was recorded (set when terminal input was received), the elapsed time from that timestamp to the current CLOCK_MONOTONIC time is computed in microseconds and stored on the REPL context. The start timestamp is cleared after each measurement.
behavior.legacy-rendering-fallback: If the current agent has no layer cake (used in test mode), a combined legacy renderer is invoked directly with the scrollback and viewport parameters, bypassing the layer cake entirely.
behavior.debug-info-recording: The viewport offset, first visible document row, viewport height, and document height are stored on the REPL context for display by the debug viewport layer.
